item.title: September & October 2025 Events
item.description:
Contribution and connection are core beliefs that shape my artistic value. I’ve always believed that art and music transcend boundaries—they speak directly to the heart and bring peopel together. I’m truly excited to share three upcoming community—focused series I’ll be performing in this fall, all of which are free and open to the public.

On September 12 – 13, I’m honored to join a residency at Hamilton College in Clinton, NY titled Art Under Pressure: Repression of Music Under Stalin & Echoes Today, exploring how music survived and evolved under political repression. Highlights include a panel discussion with scholars of Russian literature and history (Sept. 12) and a multimedia concert (Sept. 13), where I’ll perform Weinberg’s Piano Quintet, Op. 18 alongside violinists Shem Guibbory, Edgar Tumajyan, cellist Andrey Tchekmazov, and pianist Sar-Shalom Strong.

Back at home in Yunlin, Taiwan on September 27, I’m excited to join a group of young Taiwanese musicians for Harmonic Heritage & Starlit Brilliance, a festive concert celebrating timeless works from opera and chamber music. It’s a joy to collaborate again with pianist Chiao-Han Liao and violinist Hsuan-Hao Hsu—fellow Curtis alumni—as well as clarinetist Chao-Chih Chen and cellist Tzu-Wei Huang from my New York musical family.

This October, I’ll be part of Crafting Friendships, a month-long exhibition in Scranton, PA, curated by Moscow Clayworks, that celebrates long-standing artistic and personal connections in the Scranton community. I’ll perform two mini solo viola recitals at the opening receptions on October 3 in each gallery, featuring music by living composer friends - Garth Knox, Daniel Temkin, Che-Yi Lee, and Dave Soldier—dedicated to the spirit of the art show: friendships through art.

item.title: Celebrating my first website!!!! 🎉
item.description:
Having a personal website has been a long-time dream of mine. As a musician, technology often felt distant and unfamiliar. Earlier this spring, my dear friend Wei—a brilliant AI scientist—generously offered to help me build this custom site. A few weeks of coding later, here it is: my freshly launched website.

Thank you, Wei, for being a wonderful friend and the guiding light in my new coding journey. I hope everyone visiting enjoys it as much as I do.

item.title: 2025 Summer Season (July - August)
item.description:
I’m excited to be returning to the Chamber Music Conference (CMC) at Hamilton, NY as a faculty member for Week 1, from July 6 to July 13. Alongside a full week of teaching, I’ll be performing Haydn’s String Quartet in C Major, Op. 20, No. 2 on Wednesday, July 9, with fellow faculty members Yezu Woo, Mayuki Fukuhara, and Laura Metcalf. I’m really looking forward to making music together and reconnecting with the CMC community.

In August, I’ll be heading to Taiwan for two chamber music concerts—in Tainan and Taipei—presented by the Wan Sha Performing Arts Center. Back when I was a student at the Curtis Institute of Music, I performed Taneyev’s Piano Quintet in G Minor, Op. 30 with my friend and wonderful violinist, Hsuan-Hao Hsu. We were both so inspired by the piece that we promised to play it again someday in our home country. Now, twelve years later, that promise is finally coming true!

I’m especially excited to collaborate for the first time with Simon Thompson, principal cellist of the National Symphony Orchestra (Taiwan), and pianist Oscar Lin, assistant professor at the National Taipei University of Education. I’ll also be performing again with the fantastic violinist Hsin-Yun Wu.

I’m truly looking forward to these musical moments this summer—can’t wait to share them with you!
